sprite

builtins.cpp

Go to the documentation of this file.
00001 
00005 #include "sprite/builtins.hpp"
00006 #include "sprite/exec.hpp"
00007 
00008 // ====== Guards ======
00009 
00010 namespace
00011 {
00012   using namespace sprite;
00013 
00014   // The guard function determines which operands to a builtin operation are
00015   // valid.
00016 
00018   struct NoGuard
00019   {
00020     NoGuard() {}
00021     // Unary
00022     template<typename Arg> bool operator()(Arg const &) const { return true; }
00023     // Binary
00024     template<typename Lhs, typename Rhs>
00025       bool operator()(Lhs const &, Rhs const &) const { return true; }
00026   };
00027 
00029   struct DivGuard
00030   {
00031     DivGuard() {}
00032     template<typename Lhs, typename Rhs>
00033       bool operator()(Lhs const &, Rhs const & rhs) const { return rhs != 0; }
00034   };
00035 
00037   template<BuiltinOp Op> struct GuardOf : mpl::identity<NoGuard> {};
00038   template<> struct GuardOf<OP_INT_DIV> : mpl::identity<DivGuard> {};
00039   template<> struct GuardOf<OP_FLOAT_DIV> : mpl::identity<DivGuard> {};
00040 
00041   // ====== UnguardedExecute ======
00042 
00044   template<BuiltinOp Op> struct UnguardedExecute;
00045   #define SPRITE_DEFINE_OP(builtin_op,op)                \
00046       template<> struct UnguardedExecute<builtin_op>     \
00047       {                                                  \
00048         UnguardedExecute() {}                            \
00049                                                          \
00050         template<typename T>                             \
00051         T operator()(T const & lhs, T const & rhs) const \
00052           { return (lhs op rhs); }                       \
00053       };                                                 \
00054       
00055 
00056   SPRITE_DEFINE_OP(OP_INT_ADD,+)
00057   SPRITE_DEFINE_OP(OP_INT_SUB,-)
00058   SPRITE_DEFINE_OP(OP_INT_MUL,*)
00059   SPRITE_DEFINE_OP(OP_INT_DIV,/)
00060   SPRITE_DEFINE_OP(OP_FLOAT_ADD,+)
00061   SPRITE_DEFINE_OP(OP_FLOAT_SUB,-)
00062   SPRITE_DEFINE_OP(OP_FLOAT_MUL,*)
00063   SPRITE_DEFINE_OP(OP_FLOAT_DIV,/)
00064 
00065   #undef SPRITE_DEFINE_OP
00066 
00067   // ====== GuardedExecute ======
00068 
00074   template<BuiltinOp Op> struct GuardedExecute
00075   {
00076     GuardedExecute() {}
00077 
00078     template<typename T>
00079     void operator()(Node & out, T const & lhs, T const & rhs) const
00080     {
00081       static typename GuardOf<Op>::type const guard;
00082       static UnguardedExecute<Op> const exec;
00083 
00084       if(guard(lhs,rhs))
00085         { rewrite<meta::TagValueOf<Op>::value>()(out, exec(lhs,rhs)); }
00086       else
00087         { rewrite_fail(out); }
00088     }
00089   };
00090 
00091   // ====== BuiltinImpl ======
00092 
00094   template<BuiltinOp Op> struct BuiltinImpl : static_visitor<void>
00095   {
00096     BuiltinImpl(Node & node) : m_out(node) {}
00097   private:
00098     Node & m_out;
00099 
00101     typedef typename meta::TagValueOf<Op>::type TagType;
00102     typedef typename meta::NodeOf<TagType::value>::type NodeType;
00103   public:
00105     void operator()(NodeType const & lhs, NodeType const & rhs) const
00106     {
00107       static GuardedExecute<Op> const exec;
00108       exec(m_out, lhs.payload.value, rhs.payload.value);
00109     }
00110 
00112     template<typename Lhs, typename Rhs>
00113     void operator()(Lhs const &, Rhs const &) const
00114     {
00115       throw RuntimeError(
00116           "type error while executing " BOOST_PP_STRINGIZE(op)
00117         );
00118     }
00119   };
00120 
00121   // ====== Dispatch ======
00122 
00128   template<BuiltinOp Op, size_t Arity=meta::ArityOf<Op>::value>
00129     struct Dispatch;
00130 
00132   template<BuiltinOp Op> struct Dispatch<Op,1>
00133   {
00134     void operator()(Node & node)
00135     {
00136       assert(node.arity() == 1);
00137       return visit(BuiltinImpl<Op>(node), *node[0]);
00138     }
00139   };
00140 
00142   template<BuiltinOp Op> struct Dispatch<Op,2>
00143   {
00144     void operator()(Node & node)
00145     {
00146       assert(node.arity() == 2);
00147       return visit(BuiltinImpl<Op>(node), *node[0], *node[1]);
00148     }
00149   };
00150 }
00151 
00152 namespace sprite
00153 {
00154   // ====== h_func ======
00155 
00157   template<BuiltinOp Op> void h_func(Node & node)
00158   {
00159     // H.4 TODO
00160     // Q. can this just check against CTOR?
00161     // Q. how can this be needed?  The only call is from head_normalize.
00162     if(is_ctor(node.tag())) return;
00163   
00164     BOOST_FOREACH(NodePtr & child, node.iter())
00165     {
00166       switch(child->tag())
00167       {
00168         // H.1 TODO
00169         case CHOICE: return pull_tab(node, child);
00170         // H.5 TODO
00171         case FAIL: return rewrite_fail(node);
00172         // H.2 TODO
00173         case OPER: return head_normalize(*child);
00174         default:;
00175       }
00176     }
00177   
00178     #ifndef NDEBUG
00179     BOOST_FOREACH(NodePtr const & child, node.iter())
00180       { assert(is_builtin(child->tag())); }
00181     #endif
00182   
00183     // H.3 TODO
00184     // Compute the result and rewrite the node.
00185     Dispatch<Op>()(node);
00186   }
00187 
00189   h_func_type builtin_h[OP_END] =
00190   {
00191       &h_func<OP_INT_ADD>
00192     , &h_func<OP_INT_SUB>
00193     , &h_func<OP_INT_MUL>
00194     , &h_func<OP_INT_DIV>
00195     , &h_func<OP_FLOAT_ADD>
00196     , &h_func<OP_FLOAT_SUB>
00197     , &h_func<OP_FLOAT_MUL>
00198     , &h_func<OP_FLOAT_DIV>
00199   };
00200 
00201   // std::string builtin_ctor[C_END] = {};
00202   std::string builtin_oper[OP_END] = { "+", "-", "*", "/", "+", "-", "*", "/" };
00203 }
